perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which is designated as 'confidential' o r – where communicated orally – is designated as confidential or referred to similarly when communic ated. Oral communications must be confirmed subsequently in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which is designated as 'confidential'. Oral communications do not need to be confirmed subsequently in writing to be considered confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original requirement that oral communications must be confirmed in writing to be considered confidential. This creates ambiguity around what qualifies as confidential information, particularly when communicated orally. The change impacts all sections relying on the definition of 'confidential information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject matter of non-disclosure agreement For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which is designated as 'confidential'. Oral communications do not need to be confirmed subsequently in writing to be considered confidential. 01/2016 2/5 The term 'confidential information' includes, but i s not limited to, all documents, drawings, data, subjects, information, records, knowhow, protected rights, inventions, ideas, concepts, knowledge, technology, design, specifications, including dimen sions, tolerances, materials and connections, blueprints, diagrams, software, improvements, produ ction procedures, production processes, formulae, all documents and information concerning the company, in particular administrative and commercial facts and details concerning customers, products, suppliers and other business partners and any and all documents and information insofar a s it relates to the collaboration proposed which is disclosed during the term of the contract and is accessible only to a limited circle of persons or where the DISCLOSER intends that it should only be available to a limited circle of persons, however that information may be disclosed, e.g. in writing, orally, by telephone, encoded, presented in graphi c form, etc. and whatever the media and recording pro cess used, e.g. optically, electronically or magnetically. The DISCLOSEE for the purposes of this agreement i s the DISCLOSEE as defined above.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires oral communications to be confirmed in writing to be considered confidential, while the changed text explicitly states that oral communications do not need to be confirmed in writing. The contradicted text uses the definition created by the change, but there is still a clear contradiction between the original definition and the contradicted text/changed text, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability. This meets the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The DISCLOSEE agrees to keep any and all written, e lectronic and oral information and models conveyed or made available to it directly or indire ctly in the course of the collaboration strictly in confidence and not to disclose it or make it availa ble to any third party without the DISCLOSER's consent in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE agrees to keep any and all written and electronic information and models conveyed or made available to it directly or indirectly in the course of the collaboration strictly in confidence and not to disclose it or make it available to any third party without the DISCLOSER's consent in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'oral information' from this clause, it is now unclear if the DISCLOSEE is obligated to keep oral information confidential. This contradicts the previously defined term of 'confidential information' which includes oral information, creating an internal inconsistency regarding the scope of confidentiality. The change causes contradiction in section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As with the confidential information, no results ob tained from that information may be disclosed to any third parties or made available otherwise witho ut the DISCLOSER's consent in writing. This also applies without limitations to any possible termina tion of the business relationship between both parties. 01/2016 3/5
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'oral information' in the definition of confidential information. The changed text removes 'oral information,' creating ambiguity about whether oral information is protected. The contradicted text refers to 'confidential information' without specifying the type, implying it includes all previously defined types (including oral). Therefore, the modified text contradicts the contradicted text by creating uncertainty about the protection of oral information, which was previously and implicitly covered under the definition of 'confidential information.' This aligns with the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition. It alters the scope of what is considered confidential, creating a direct conflict in how the agreement is enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The DISCLOSEE may not make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation other than to staff who necessarily re quire it to perform their duties. Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE may make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation to staff who necessarily require it to perform their duties. It is recommended, but not required, that Such persons be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers due to intentional breaches only.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by making it optional for the DISCLOSEE to ensure their staff are bound by confidentiality agreements, weakening the confidentiality obligations. Additionally, it limits the DISCLOSEE's liability to only intentional breaches. This contradicts the general intent to maintain strict confidentiality as outlined in other sections. The change impacts section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential inform ation provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE cannot deduce any rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of ap plying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes confidentiality optional and limits liability to intentional breaches only, contradicting the contradicted text which states that 'any and all' confidential information is the property of Fronius, implying strict and comprehensive protection. The change weakens the original strong obligation of confidentiality and the associated liability, creating a clear conflict with the intent and scope of the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: As with the confidential information, no results ob tained from that information may be disclosed to any third parties or made available otherwise witho ut the DISCLOSER's consent in writing. This also applies without limitations to any possible termina tion of the business relationship between both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Results obtained from the confidential information may be disclosed to third parties with the DISCLOSER's verbal consent. This does not apply after termination of the business relationship between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original agreement, allowing verbal consent for disclosure, while previously requiring written consent. It also limits the restriction on disclosure to the duration of the business relationship. It is contradicting in section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all documents containing confidential infor mation, copies of such documents and extracts the DISCLOSEE makes from such documents and models the DISCLOSER provides must be returned or demonstrably destroyed and their destru ction be confirmed in writing should the DISCLOSER so require.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it allows verbal consent for disclosing confidential information, whereas the original text required written consent. The contradicted text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information, implying a strict control and protection of this information, which is undermined by the changed text's allowance of verbal consent for disclosure. Additionally, the changed text limits the restriction on disclosure to the duration of the business relationship, further weakening the protection of confidential information after termination, conflicting with the intention of the contradicted section to ensure its secure return or destruction regardless of the relationship's status.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential inform ation provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE cannot deduce any rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of ap plying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.changed_text: Any rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential information provided become joint property of Fronius and the DISCLOSEE. The DISCLOSEE can deduce rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of applying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the ownership of rights related to confidential information from solely Fronius to joint ownership, and it allows the DISCLOSEE to potentially use the DISCLOSER's information against them in protected rights applications. This fundamentally alters the agreement's intention and creates a contradiction. It is in contradiction with section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Documents which contain confidential information mu st not be reproduced or extracts made from them without the DISCLOSER's prior consent in writi ng. Models sent must not be analysed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the ownership of confidential information rights and allows the DISCLOSEE to use the DISCLOSER's information against them. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits the DISCLOSEE from reproducing confidential information or analyzing models without the DISCLOSER's consent. The changed text now permits actions that the contradicted text explicitly forbids, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all documents containing confidential infor mation, copies of such documents and extracts the DISCLOSEE makes from such documents and models the DISCLOSER provides must be returned or demonstrably destroyed and their destru ction be confirmed in writing should the DISCLOSER so require.
perturbation.changed_text: Documents containing confidential information, copies of such documents, and extracts the DISCLOSEE makes from such documents need not be returned or destroyed unless the DISCLOSER provides compensation for their initial receipt and handling. Confirmation of destruction is not required.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly alters the DISCLOSEE's obligation to return or destroy confidential information, now requiring compensation from the DISCLOSER. This weakens the DISCLOSER's control over their confidential information and introduces a financial element that was not previously present, contradicting the core principle of non-disclosure, in section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: These obligations will not apply, or will cease to apply, to using information where the DISCLOSEE can show that that information / Passed into the public domain without breaching th ese obligations; / Was already in the DISCLOSEE's possession before t he DISCLOSER disclosed it to it. Information will be deemed to be confidential infor mation for the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement even if the confidential information come s under one or more of these exceptions only in part, provided the confidential information itself does not come under one or more of these exceptions in its entirety.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information, whereas the changed text introduces a condition of compensation from the DISCLOSER for the return or destruction, effectively giving the DISCLOSEE an option to retain the confidential information if compensation is not provided. This contradicts the non-disclosure principle as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, creating an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text outlines exceptions where obligations do not apply, but does not mention a compensation clause. Therefore, the changed text alters obligations, causing a strong contradiction.
